name: Adire Oodua Textile Hub
tagline: Hand-dyed indigo from the heart of Abeokuta
story: A women-led cooperative in Itoku, Abeokuta carrying forward five generations of Yoruba indigo dye craft. Every yard is hand-resisted with raffia, stitched, or stencil-cut before its dance through the indigo vat.

name: Kano Kembe Weavers
story: Operating out of the historic Kofar Mata dye pits and Dala weaving floors, Kano Kembe Weavers spins, dyes, and looms cotton-blend kembe in earth tones favoured by Nigerian designers reinterpreting Hausa heritage.

name: Aso Iwòfà Looms
tagline: Hand-loomed Aso-oke for ceremony and runway
story: Aso Iwòfà runs nine traditional pit looms in Ede, Osun State, producing hand-loomed Aso-oke strips in classic etu, sanyan, and alaari, plus modern metallic blends commissioned by Lagos couture houses.

name: Akwete Mma
tagline: Igbo handwoven Akwete cloth, Abia State
story: Akwete Mma is a guild of women weavers in Akwete town, Abia State, the historic home of the Akwete cloth. Every metre is hand-picked on a vertical loom — geometric motifs, ikaki tortoise patterns, and modern colour stories.

name: Balogun Textiles
tagline: Lagos Island's go-to for premium Ankara & wax prints
story: A Balogun Market institution since 1989, stocking and sub-printing wax-resist Ankara across over 200 motifs. They carry African and Dutch wax basecloths, plus a small-batch line dyed in Lagos.

name: Onitsha Lace House
tagline: French and Swiss lace, hand-finished in Onitsha
story: Onitsha Lace House imports cordlace, sequined tulle, and Austrian lace, then hand-finishes each bolt in their workshop above Onitsha Main Market — adding bead, stone, or cut-out detailing on demand.

name: Kente Bridge
tagline: Ghanaian Kente weavers, supplying Lagos studios
story: A Lagos–Kumasi link bringing Bonwire-woven Kente strip cloth to Nigerian designers. Kente Bridge handles import, customs, and domestic delivery so designers can order Kente in single-yard quantities.

name: Aba Mills Co.
tagline: Aba's industrial mill turning out cotton, denim & linen
story: Aba Mills runs a mid-scale weaving plant on the Enugu-Aba expressway, supplying ready-stock cotton, midweight denim, and a growing local linen line — preferred by ready-to-wear labels needing reliable bolts.

name: Ibadan Dye Works
story: A small-batch dye house in Bodija, Ibadan, taking blank cotton and silk and over-dyeing it with natural indigo, kola, and laali. Designers send white goods or buy off-the-vat colourways.

name: Lagos Linen Lab
tagline: Tropical-weight linen woven for Lagos heat
story: Lagos Linen Lab focuses on breathable linen and cotton-linen blends in muted, sun-bleached palettes. Their bolts have become a staple for resortwear and minimalist menswear labels in Victoria Island.
